title: Lanes & Turns
description: Choosing lanes, turning, changing lanes, and roundabouts.

scenarios.prompt: You're about to turn left. Which lane should you be in?

feedback.correct: Yes — move into the left lane before a left turn.
feedback.right: The right lane is for right turns and going straight. Use the left lane to turn left. Try again.
feedback.any: Get into the correct lane early — left lane for a left turn. Try again.
feedback.default: Use the left lane to turn left. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You are turning left onto a road with two lanes going your way. Which lane should you turn into?

choices.label: The left lane, nearest the center line
The right lane, nearest the curb

feedback.correct: Yes — turn into the lane closest to the one you came from. On a left turn that's the left lane.
feedback.far: Crossing to the far lane during a turn is risky. Turn into the nearest lane — the left one. Try again.
feedback.any: Turn into the nearest lane, not just any lane. For a left turn, that's the left lane. Try again.
feedback.shoulder: The shoulder isn't a driving lane. Turn into the left lane, nearest the center. Try again.
feedback.default: Turn into the nearest lane — the left lane for a left turn. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: The middle lane has yellow arrows that curve left from both sides. What is this lane for?

choices.label: Making left turns from either direction

feedback.correct: Yes — the center lane is a shared left-turn lane. Cars from both directions use it to turn left.
feedback.pass: Never pass in the center turn lane. It's only for making left turns. Try again.
feedback.thru: It's not a driving lane — it's only for turning left. Try again.
feedback.park: You can't park there. The center lane is only for making left turns. Try again.
feedback.default: The center lane is only for making left turns. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You want to turn left into a driveway. How should you use the center two-way left-turn lane?

choices.label: Move into it just before your turn
Drive in it for several blocks first
Use it to pass the car ahead
Treat it like a regular driving lane

feedback.correct: Yes — enter the center lane only a short distance before your turn, then turn when it's clear.
feedback.blocks: Don't travel far in it. Move in only just before your turn. Try again.
feedback.pass: Never use the center turn lane to pass. Use it only to turn left. Try again.
feedback.regular: It's not a regular lane. Enter it only just before a left turn. Try again.
feedback.default: Move into the center lane only just before your turn. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: A separate left-turn lane with a white arrow opens up before the intersection. You're turning left. What should you do?

choices.label: Signal and move into the turn lane early
Turn left from the through lane instead
Cut into it at the last second
Straddle both lanes until you turn

feedback.correct: Yes — signal, then move into the dedicated turn lane early so you don't slow the through traffic.
feedback.through: Use the marked turn lane, not the through lane. Move over early. Try again.
feedback.last: Cutting in late is dangerous. Signal and move into the turn lane early. Try again.
feedback.straddle: Pick one lane — move fully into the turn lane. Try again.
feedback.default: Signal and move into the dedicated turn lane early. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: You want to change lanes. What should you do first?

choices.label: Signal, then check your mirror and blind spot

feedback.correct: Yes — signal first, then check your mirrors and look over your shoulder before moving.
feedback.quick: Always signal and check your blind spot before moving over. Try again.
feedback.speed: Don't force it — signal, check, then move when there's a safe gap. Try again.
feedback.movefirst: Signal and check first, then move — not the other way around. Try again.
feedback.default: Signal and check your blind spot first. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: A car is in your blind spot — the area beside you that your mirrors can't show. Before you change lanes, what should you do?

choices.label: Look over your shoulder to check
Speed up so no one is beside you

feedback.correct: Yes — your mirrors can't show the spot right beside you. Always look over your shoulder before you change lanes.
feedback.mirrors: Mirrors miss the blind spot beside you. Look over your shoulder too. Try again.
feedback.honk: Honking doesn't make it safe — check your blind spot by looking over your shoulder first. Try again.
feedback.speed: Speeding up is risky. Look over your shoulder to check your blind spot. Try again.
feedback.default: Look over your shoulder to check your blind spot. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Which way do you drive around a roundabout?

choices.label: Enter to the right and go around left (counter-clockwise)

feedback.correct: Yes — you always go counter-clockwise, keeping to the right as you enter.
feedback.left: In the US you keep right and travel counter-clockwise. Try again.
feedback.straight: You drive around the circle, not across it. Try again.
feedback.clockwise: In the US you go counter-clockwise, not clockwise. Try again.
feedback.default: Go around to the right, counter-clockwise. Try again.
